entries.title: Recorded real Codex terminal work beside the live preview
entries.summary: Pixelbox now renders terminal output in the recorded visual loop and drives real Codex through keyboard input, so the proof video shows terminal activity and preview changes together.
points: Restored visible terminal text rendering for the browser-backed terminal path used by visual-loop tests
Added keyboard and paste handling so Playwright can type commands into the visible terminal instead of writing directly to the backend
Updated the real-Codex visual-loop probe to record terminal-entered Codex commands alongside the preview updates they produce

entries.title: Added visual-loop video review for frontend verification
entries.summary: Pixelbox now pairs screenshot-driven iteration with a video-review skill and Playwright probe, so visual changes can be checked against recorded evidence instead of code diffs alone.
points: Added a Pixelbox screenshot-loop workflow for capturing, inspecting, patching, and recapturing rendered UI changes
Added a Pixelbox video-review skill for extracting frames from recordings and judging whether a workflow actually worked
Added a Playwright visual-loop video probe that records the workspace, captures numbered screenshots, and verifies preview capture-region updates

entries.title: Added screenshot-driven visual iteration for the native workspace
entries.summary: Pixelbox now publishes the live preview viewport bounds and includes a local capture workflow so agents can verify frontend changes against the actual native window.
points: Added preview capture-region APIs so the renderer can report the active preview geometry to the local backend
Added a macOS window capture script and Pixelbox screenshot skill for real rendered UI review loops
Documented the capture workflow for saving window or preview screenshots with matching metadata

entries.title: Restored native Codex relaunch and project terminal persistence
entries.summary: Pixelbox now relaunches Codex correctly inside the native Ghostty terminal path again, with project-aware session startup restored after the Zig and Zero Native migration.
points: Fixed native terminal startup so Codex launches from the embedded Ghostty host instead of falling back to a blank login shell
Restored per-project native terminal continuity so project switching no longer drops the active agent session model
Added a dedicated native terminal regression probe to verify the live panel state and startup payload contract

entries.title: Moved Pixelbox onto a native Zig desktop runtime with Ghostty
entries.summary: Pixelbox now runs through Zig and Zero Native, with Ghostty taking over the terminal surface and a more polished translucent desktop shell replacing the older layered approach.
points: Migrated the active desktop app flow from the older Electron path to a Zig-powered Zero Native runtime
Integrated a native Ghostty terminal panel with titleless macOS chrome and tuned translucent styling
Cleaned up legacy Electron and xterm package usage so the shipped path matches the current product

entries.title: Added agent monitoring, project recovery, and smarter local preview orchestration
entries.summary: Pixelbox moved further from a polished shell into a more durable local software workspace with better visibility into active agents and fewer broken localhost loops.
points: Added an in-app agent monitor for active Codex sessions with backend inspection hooks
Introduced hidden-project restore controls and cleaner project action menus
Improved runtime startup by automatically reassigning colliding localhost ports and injecting the matching PORT environment variable

entries.title: Expanded AI launch flows and native runtime support
entries.summary: Pixelbox gained wider CLI startup coverage plus the ability to manage non-web preview workflows without forcing everything through a browser tab.
points: Added AI Launch presets for Codex, Claude, Gemini, Hermes, OpenClaw TUI, and plain terminal mode
Added native app runtime support with run, capture, and image configuration fields
Added project switching shortcuts and terminal drag-and-drop for faster local workflow control

entries.title: Started the dedicated Pixelbox landing page
entries.summary: Pixelbox now has its own product-facing page instead of living only as a repo or a mention on the personal site.
points: Positioned Pixelbox as a standalone Pixel Claw Labs product
Added a public changelog surface for ongoing release notes
Set up the site to deploy cleanly from a Vercel subdirectory

entries.title: Tightened the local workspace loop
entries.summary: Pixelbox continued shifting from a simple desktop shell into a project-aware software workspace.
points: Project switching and runtime continuity were stabilized
Preview navigation and runtime controls became more reliable
Desktop runtime and Playwright flows expanded to cover long-session behavior
